title: Bowl & Scoop Mix
description: Put a few soft toys, wooden spoons, and stacking cups in a big bowl. Let them scoop, dump, and mix everything around.

title: Texture Basket
description: Fill a basket with 4-5 household items of very different textures — a soft sock, a wooden spoon, a silicone spatula, a bit of bubble wrap — for touch exploration.

title: Peekaboo with a Scarf
description: Use a light scarf or muslin cloth to play peekaboo, covering your face and theirs.

title: Stacking Cups
description: Nest and stack plastic cups or small tupperware into a tower, then knock it down together.

title: Crinkle Paper Play
description: Give them old newspaper or tissue paper to scrunch, tear, and crinkle. Sit with them since paper bits are a mouthing risk.

title: Pots & Pans Drum Circle
description: Pull out pots, pans, and a wooden spoon and let them bang out a rhythm.

title: Ball Drop Box
description: Cut a hole in an empty tissue box and let them drop small balls or pom-poms in and shake them out.

title: Mirror Time
description: Sit together in front of an unbreakable mirror and point out facial features, expressions, and reflections.

title: Pouring Station
description: Set up two bowls and a cup, and let them practice pouring dry rice or lentils between them over a tray. Stay close, this is a choking hazard if unsupervised.

title: Sock Matching
description: Dump the clean laundry basket and have them find and pair up matching socks.

title: Cardboard Box Tunnel
description: Open both ends of a large empty box and let them crawl through it like a tunnel.

title: Sticker Peeling
description: Give them a sheet of stickers and a piece of paper. Peeling and sticking is great for fine motor skills.

title: Kitchen Utensil Sort
description: Give them a pile of spoons and forks and two bowls, and ask them to sort which goes where.

title: Laundry Basket Ride
description: Sit them in an empty laundry basket and gently pull it around a carpeted room like a sled.

title: Tape Trail Walk
description: Stick a line of masking tape on the floor in a wiggly path and have them walk or toddle along it.

title: Flour Dough Squish
description: Mix flour, water, and a little oil into a soft dough. Let them squish, roll, and poke it on a tray.

title: Pretend Kitchen
description: Set out pots, empty food containers, and a wooden spoon so they can 'cook' a pretend meal.

title: Color Hunt
description: Hold up a colored object and ask them to run and find three more things around the house in that color.

title: Button Sort by Size
description: Sort buttons or large beads into the cups of a muffin tin by size or color. Supervise closely, small parts are a choking hazard.

title: Indoor Obstacle Course
description: Use couch cushions, chairs, and tape lines to build a course to crawl under, jump over, and balance along.

title: Paper Plate Faces
description: Give them paper plates and markers or glued-on scraps to draw silly faces on.

title: Counting Steps
description: Walk around the house together counting steps out loud from room to room.

title: Shadow Puppets
description: Dim the lights, grab a flashlight or lamp, and make hand-shape shadows dance on the wall.

title: Wet Sponge Painting
description: Dip a sponge in water and 'paint' shapes and letters on the floor tiles or driveway. It dries on its own, no mess to clean.

title: Picture Scavenger Hunt
description: Draw or write a simple list of 5 household items and send them off to find each one.

title: Blanket Fort Building
description: Use couch cushions, blankets, and chairs to build a fort, then let them decorate the inside.

title: Paper Airplane Contest
description: Fold a couple of paper airplanes together, then take turns flying them and measuring the distance in footsteps.

title: Alphabet Object Hunt
description: Pick a letter and race to find something around the house that starts with it. Repeat with a few letters.

title: Sock Puppet Show
description: Turn old socks into puppets with markers or glued-on buttons, then put on a two-minute show for the family.

title: Balance Beam Walk
description: Lay a rope or line of tape on the floor and practice walking heel-to-toe along it without stepping off.

title: Sink or Float Test
description: Fill a bowl with water and test which household objects sink and which float, guessing before each one.

title: Cardboard Ramp Race
description: Tape a piece of cardboard into a ramp and race balls or toy cars down it, testing different angles.

title: Board Game Marathon
description: Pull out a family board game or deck of cards and play a couple of rounds together.

title: Tidy-Up Race
description: Set a timer and turn cleaning up a room into a race against the clock.

title: Nature Sketch Journal
description: Sit by a window or step outside and sketch or describe three things they see — a leaf, a bird, a cloud shape.

title: No-Bake Recipe Helper
description: Let them measure and mix ingredients for a simple no-bake snack like energy balls or a fruit salad.

title: Origami Basics
description: Follow step-by-step folds to make a simple paper boat or hat.

title: Indoor Bowling
description: Line up empty plastic bottles and roll a soft ball to knock them down.

title: Letter to Someone Special
description: Write or draw a short letter to a grandparent or friend and decorate the envelope.

title: String Telephone Test
description: Poke a hole in two paper cups, thread a string between them, and test how sound travels when the string is tight versus loose.

title: Tallest Tower Challenge
description: Set a 15-minute timer and see how tall a tower they can build with building blocks or stacked household items before it topples.

title: Coin Collection Sort
description: Sort a jar of loose coins by year, type, or country and count up the totals.

title: DIY Comic Strip
description: Fold a sheet of paper into 6 panels and draw a short comic strip with a beginning, middle, and end.

title: Pretend Shop Budgeting
description: Set up a pretend shop with household items and play money, and have them add up totals and give change.

title: Knot Tying Practice
description: Teach them 3 basic knots using a shoelace or rope, then have them try tying them from memory.

title: Cardboard City
description: Turn recycled boxes into buildings for a mini cardboard town, complete with roads drawn on paper.

title: Card Game Tournament
description: Teach them a new card game like rummy or UNO and run a mini tournament.

title: Baking Soda Volcano
description: Build a small volcano shape from clay or paper, then mix baking soda and vinegar inside a tray to watch it erupt.

title: Solo Cooking Challenge
description: Have them plan and cook one simple dish on their own from a recipe, with you nearby for stove or knife safety.

title: Room Redesign Sketch
description: Have them measure their room and sketch a redesigned layout on paper, planning where furniture would go.

title: Reflective Journaling
description: Give them a notebook and a few open-ended prompts to write about — a memory, a goal, a person they admire.

title: No-Sew T-Shirt Tote Bag
description: Turn an old t-shirt into a tote bag by cutting and knotting the fabric, no sewing required.

title: Strategy Game Night
description: Play a strategy game like chess, checkers, or a jigsaw puzzle together or solo.

title: Design-Your-Own Workout
description: Have them design a bodyweight exercise circuit (push-ups, squats, planks) and time themselves through it.

title: Letter to Future Self
description: Write a letter to open in a year — goals, favorite things right now, predictions — then seal it in an envelope.

title: Plan a Mock Trip
description: Have them plan an imaginary trip on paper within a set budget, researching and listing costs for travel, stay, and food.
